title: Portering Supervisor
how_to_become: Apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website. A working knowledge of portering services in a hospital is essential.
description: Portering supervisors coordinate portering activities in healthcare settings, ensuring patients are transferred safely, medicines and biological samples are moved correctly, medical equipment is relocated, clean linen is delivered, and clinical waste is transferred safely for disposal.
role_overview: As a portering supervisor, you'll maintain the smooth operation of the portering services team, allocating staff and resources and providing an efficient level of service to all hospital departments. You'll organise rotas, prioritise incoming tasks, and assign porters.

entry_requirements.overview: A good standard of education and a working knowledge of portering services in a hospital setting are essential. No specific entry requirements.

work_experience: Work placements and volunteering can provide healthcare experience and help when applying for a new job with NHSScotland.

training_areas: COSHH — Control of Substances Hazardous to Health
additional_qualifications: SVQ Facilities Management at SCQF Level 7
IOSH Health & Safety awarded by the Institute of Occupational Safety and Health

chunks.text: To become a portering supervisor, apply for a vacancy on the NHS recruitment website. A working knowledge of hospital portering services is essential.
Portering supervisors coordinate portering activities ensuring safe transfer of patients, medicines, biological samples, medical equipment, clean linen, and clinical waste across healthcare settings.
Training includes COSHH, mandatory training, health and safety, and risk management. Additional qualifications include SVQ Facilities Management at SCQF Level 7 and IOSH Health & Safety.
With qualifications and experience, portering supervisors can progress to portering manager or support services manager.
